description: Schema governing all field-originated intelligence inputs and observation objects. Field intelligence may enrich recommendations but may NOT mutate canonical assembly truth.

shop_drawing_correction.description: Correction issued against a shop drawing in the field

rfi_reference.description: Reference to a formal Request for Information tied to field observation

submittal_correction.description: Correction applied to an approved submittal based on field conditions

installer_note.description: Free-form note entered by installing contractor documenting field condition

field_fix.description: Deviation from design resolved in the field without formal RFI

observed_conflict.description: Conflict between two or more design elements observed during installation

inspection_observation.description: Formal observation recorded during a scheduled or unscheduled inspection

reality_capture_ref.description: Reference to a scan, photogrammetry model, or 360 capture documenting as-built condition

deviation_record.description: Documented deviation between design intent and installed condition

corrective_action.description: Action taken or prescribed to resolve a deviation

installer_note.description: Structured capture of installer-originated field note

review_resolution.description: Resolution record for a field observation that passed through formal review

observed_variation.description: Variation in material, dimension, or method observed versus specification

means_methods_note.description: Contractor means and methods note not subject to design review but captured for record

immutability_rules.rule_1: Field intelligence objects may enrich recommendation layers and surface deviations to the revision queue.
immutability_rules.rule_2: Field intelligence objects may NOT write to, overwrite, or delete canonical assembly truth records.
immutability_rules.rule_3: Any field input that attempts to mutate a canonical kernel MUST be rejected and logged as a truth-plane violation.
immutability_rules.rule_4: Deviation records create enrichment annotations only; they do not alter the source design record.
